sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: I can see the first green block (top-left) has changed from green (N) to light blue (S)! Clicking on a green block converts it to blue. This is the mechanic - I need to click on all green blocks to convert them to blue. Let me verify this and then click on all remaining green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Verify the change - check what the clicked block became
seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Remaining green blocks: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    print(f"  rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check the converted block area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTop-left area (rows 0-6, cols 10-30):")
for r in range(0, 7):
    print(f"  {r}: {ascii_lines[r][10:30]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
